title: 🟡 Heap (PQ) — Dry Run & Recall
oneSentence: Trace Top K Elements and Merge K Sorted Lists.

topK.title: Top K Elements — Dry Run
topK.story: VIP bouncer throws out the smallest.
topK.description: Trace the Min-Heap keeping the Top K elements.
code: public int findKthLargest(int[] nums, int k) {

quiz.q: Why do we use a Min-Heap (PriorityQueue) instead of sorting the array?
options: To keep the time complexity at O(N log K) instead of O(N log N)
Because Java arrays cannot be sorted
quiz.explain: Sorting takes O(N log N) time. By maintaining a Min-Heap of size K, pushing/popping takes O(log K), resulting in O(N log K) overall time. If K is small compared to N, this is much faster.

mergeKLists.title: Merge K Sorted Lists
mergeKLists.description: Merge k sorted linked lists and return it as one sorted list.
mergeKLists.story: Merging multiple sorted queues. Compare the front of all queues simultaneously using a Min-Heap.
code: PriorityQueue<ListNode> pq = new PriorityQueue<>((a, b) -> a.val - b.val);

steps.desc: Push head of L1 (1), L2 (1), L3 (2) into Heap.
Pop minimum (1). Append to result. Push next node (4).

quiz.q: What is the time complexity of pushing the initial heads into the heap?
quiz.explain: We push exactly K nodes (one for each list). Each push takes O(log K), so O(K log K) total for initialization.

findMedian.title: Find Median from Data Stream
findMedian.description: Design a data structure that supports adding numbers and finding the median.
findMedian.story: Two buckets balancing a scale. Small bucket (Max-Heap) holds bottom half, large bucket (Min-Heap) holds top half.
code: PriorityQueue<Integer> small = new PriorityQueue<>((a, b) -> b - a); // Max-Heap

steps.desc: Add 1: small=[1], push to large=[1], small is smaller so small=[1].
Add 2: small=[2,1], large=[2], small size < large size so small=[1], large=[2].

quiz.q: Why do we use two heaps instead of a sorted array?
options: To achieve O(log N) insertion time instead of O(N).
Because arrays cannot hold floating point medians.
quiz.explain: Inserting into a sorted array takes O(N) because you have to shift elements. Two heaps allow inserting in O(log N) while keeping the middle elements accessible in O(1).
